steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that most effectively emphasizes the location of the Haystack Mountain School of Crafts. That means location — Deer Isle, Maine — should be the central, load-bearing idea of the sentence, not a side detail.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Those wishing to see the Haystack Mountain School of Crafts in person will have to travel to Deer Isle, Maine.: The entire sentence exists to name the location — if you want to see the school, you must go to Deer Isle, Maine. Location is the point. ✓
  • Choice B — A stunning example of critical regionalist architecture, Edward Larrabee Barnes's Haystack Mountain School of Crafts features a cluster of cedar-shingled buildings.: Emphasizes architectural style and features; omits location entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — The architect responsible for designing the Haystack Mountain School of Crafts in Deer Isle, Maine, was Edward Larrabee Barnes.: Emphasizes who the architect is; Deer Isle, Maine appears only as a parenthetical detail inside a sentence about Barnes. ✗
  • Choice D — Edward Larrabee Barnes is known for designing a building complex that features a cluster of cedar-shingled buildings.: Focuses on the architect and the building's appearance; location is omitted. ✗
That is the correct answer.
